title: rumex sanguineus subsp. viridis
intro: Rumex sanguineus subsp. viridis, also known as green dock or broad-leaved dock, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Polygonaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high iron content, which has been used to treat anemia and other iron-deficiency disorders. The health benefits of Rumex sanguineus subsp. viridis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used as a food source, particularly for its leaves which can be eaten raw or cooked.
